subject: Geography
chapter: Planning and Sustainable Development in Indian Context

formulas.name: The Bruntland Commission's Definition of Sustainable Development
formulas.formula: Development that meets the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs.

formulas.name: The Precautionary Principle
formulas.formula: When there is a risk of serious or irreversible harm to the environment, the lack of full scientific certainty shall not be used as a reason for postponing cost-effective measures to prevent environmental degradation.

formulas.name: The Polluter Pays Principle
formulas.formula: Those who generate pollution should bear the costs of pollution.

formulas.name: The Rio Declaration's 27 Principles
formulas.formula: A set of guiding principles for sustainable development, including the need to ensure sustainable development, protect the environment, and promote international cooperation.
